header.title: Hazard Risk Dashboard
header.subtitle: Visualizing risk potential of natural hazards

introHtml: The Hazard Risk Dashboard delivers insights into the spatial distribution of risk from various natural hazards. Developed by HeiGIT, this tool helps visualize regions that are highly exposed or vulnerable combined with a lack of coping capacity, empowering decision-makers to identify areas requiring proactive humanitarian or disaster relief interventions.

methodology.title: Methodology
methodology.description: Access the underlying GAIA pipeline and training center.

method.title: Method
paragraphs: Our analysis pipeline leverages the power of open-source datasets and tools to quantify risk across regions. Using hazard datasets overlaid with OpenStreetMap and population data, we calculate aggregate risk scores.

The unit of analysis corresponds to sub-national administrative boundaries (e.g., ADM1 or ADM2). By computing exposure, vulnerability and coping capacity metrics, we generate a composite index of risk which is visualized across the dashboard's statistics and map components.

points.title: Hazard Exposure
points.description: Identifying regions prone to specific disaster types.

points.title: Vulnerability
points.description: Representing the sensitivity of a population to external shocks.

points.title: Coping Capacity
points.description: Measuring the ability of a population to access essential services.

limitations.title: Data Completeness
limitations.description: In regions with unmapped critical infrastructure or outdated population models, the risk may be inaccurately captured.

limitations.title: Aggregation Granularity
limitations.description: Presenting results at ADM1 or ADM2 level can obscure very localized variations in risk.

limitations.title: Static Snapshots
limitations.description: Hazards are dynamic, and static layers may not perfectly reflect ongoing or rapidly changing disaster events.

limitations.title: Lack of Local Knowledge
limitations.description: The model does not account for local knowledge and practices.

items.title: QGIS Plugin
items.description: Learn how to calculate your own risk assessment using our plugin.
